Title: Complete biosynthetic pathway of furochromones in Saposhnikovia divaricata and its evolutionary mechanism in Apiaceae plants

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots of S. divaricata; markedly higher than homologous Apiaceous PCS genes; low in petiole/leaf
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPCS catalyzes the first committed step from malonyl-CoA to noreugenin in furochromone biosynthesis. 2. Its expression is strongly correlated with furochromone accumulation (PCC > 0.95). 3. Proximal duplication and exceptionally high root expression of SdPCS are proposed to underlie abundant root furochromone accumulation in S. divaricata among Apiaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; pentaketide chromone synthase (PCS)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furochromones exhibit anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, antiviral activities; POG and 5-O-MVG are associated with treatment of respiratory virus infection, type I allergy, colitis, and aging-impaired tendon regeneration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream pathway entry gene for metabolic engineering of furochromones; likely strong target for flux enhancement and pathway stacking with SdPT, SdPC, SdCH, SdOMT, and SdUGT1/2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Suggested as initial and likely rate-limiting/key control step based on pathway position and exceptionally high expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC/LC-MS; kinetic analysis; transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana; comparative genomics/transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originated from proximal duplication of nearby SdPCS2; SdPCS2 is nearly not expressed; authors infer duplication plus high expression drove species-specific metabolite enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of noreugenin and supports high furochromone accumulation, especially in roots
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core chromone scaffold precursor for all downstream furochromones
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression highly correlated with furochromone contents (PCC > 0.95) in S. divaricata tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPT is the prenyltransferase introducing a C-6 dimethylallyl group to noreugenin. 2. It generated peucenin and represents the first reported prenyltransferase utilizing chromones as substrate. 3. Low catalytic efficiency was noted and increasing SdPT dosage improved de novo production in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential mid-upstream pathway gene for pathway reconstruction; may require overexpression/high dosage because of relatively low catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; low catalytic efficiency suggests potential bottleneck in heterologous production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome expression; in vitro enzyme assay with noreugenin and DMAPP; HPLC/LC-MS; NMR identification of product; kinetic analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SdPT2 has same catalytic function; authors increased Agrobacterium OD600 for SdPT to 0.40 to boost product yield in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables prenylation step toward peucenin, supporting downstream furochromone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate prenylated precursor for downstream cyclization and decoration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group onto C-6 of noreugenin to form peucenin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pathway bottleneck due to low catalytic efficiency

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression highly correlated with furochromone contents; lineage-specific in S. divaricata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPC catalyzes oxidative cyclization of peucenin to visamminol, establishing the furochromone ring. 2. SdPC is lineage-specific to S. divaricata among surveyed Apiaceae and no other tested homolog was effective in producing visamminol. 3. Presence of SdPC is a decisive factor for high and specific furochromone accumulation in S. divaricata.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP736 family; peucenin cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; comparative genomics; comparative transcriptomics; RNA-seq (BioProject PRJCA036214)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive furochromones with anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, antiviral activities
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical lineage-specific gene for engineering authentic furochromone production; should be included in heterologous pathway stacks
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point/essential enabling step for furochromone branch in surveyed Apiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assay with peucenin and NADPH; LC-MS; preparation and NMR confirmation of visamminol standard; comparative functional assays of homologous Apiaceous CYP736 genes; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Novel homologous enzyme function distinct from furocoumarin cyclases; SdPC could not cyclize demethylisuberosin unlike some homologs; absent from syntenic blocks of other Apiaceae examined
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of furochromone skeleton and is associated with drastic species-specific accumulation of furochromones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core furochromone intermediate required for both major products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cyclizes the prenyl side chain of peucenin to form the fused dihydrofuran ring in visamminol
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate selected by high correlation with compounds 8 and 9 (PCC > 0.95)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdCH hydroxylates C-11 of visamminol and 5-O-methylvisamminol. 2. It converts visamminol to norcimifugin and 5-O-methylvisamminol to cimifugin. 3. This enzyme supplies the hydroxylated branch required for POG formation and cimifugin-derived glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed for engineering hydroxylated furochromones and POG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome assays with NADPH; LC-MS; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on both unmethylated and methylated intermediates, indicating pathway flexibility in modification order
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes norcimifugin and cimifugin formation, enabling downstream POG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor(s) for cimifugin and POG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates C-11 of visamminol to norcimifugin and of 5-O-methylvisamminol to cimifugin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdOMT methylates the 5-OH position of visamminol and norcimifugin. 2. It converts visamminol to 5-O-methylvisamminol and norcimifugin to cimifugin. 3. SdOMT contributes to biosynthesis of both major end products including 5-O-MVG and POG branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important tailoring gene for installing the 5-methoxy moiety in both branches; valuable for reconstituting authentic product profiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assay; LC-MS; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepts both visamminol and norcimifugin, indicating methylation can occur before or after hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 5-O-methylated intermediates leading to 5-O-MVG and POG
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct intermediates for both final furochromone glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the 5-OH of visamminol and norcimifugin to form 5-O-methylvisamminol and cimifugin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed candidate UGT; expression less tightly correlated than most other pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdUGT1 (UGT93BA1) catalyzes final glucosylation of 5-O-methylvisamminol and cimifugin. 2. It produces 5-O-MVG from 5 and POG from 8, with strong preference for 11-O-glucosylation of cimifugin. 3. SdUGT1 shows higher catalytic efficiency than SdUGT2 and likely contributes strongly to terminal glycoside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; UGT93BA1; GT-B fold inferred for family
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive glucosylated furochromones with medicinal activities
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred terminal glycosyltransferase for producing authentic glucosides in chassis organisms due to higher catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for glycoside formation, but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assay with UDP-Glc; LC-MS/MS; comparison with reference standards; kinetic analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalyzes 3'-OH glucosylation of 5 and 11-OH glucosylation of 8, but not 3'-O-glucosylation of 8; higher catalytic efficiency with 8 than 5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms terminal glycosides 5-O-MVG and POG
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 3'-OH of 5-O-methylvisamminol to form 5-O-MVG and to the 11-OH of cimifugin to form POG
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed candidate UGT; expression less tightly correlated than most other pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdUGT2 (UGT93BB1) catalyzes the same terminal glucosylation reactions as SdUGT1. 2. Structural and docking analyses explain its preference for 11-O-glucosylation of cimifugin over 3'-O-glucosylation. 3. SdUGT2 is less efficient than SdUGT1 for target product formation but still supports pathway completion.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq (BioProject PRJCA036214); crystal structure PDB 8ZNK
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive glucosylated furochromones with medicinal activities
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative terminal UGT for pathway engineering; structural information may enable protein engineering of regioselectivity/efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for glycoside formation, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; kinetic analysis; X-ray crystal structure; docking; alanine scanning; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PDB ID 8ZNK; His32 implicated in catalysis; prefers 11-O-glycosylation due to more favorable interactions; does not support further 3'-O-glycosylation of 9
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms terminal glycosides 5-O-MVG and POG, with lower efficiency than SdUGT1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 3'-OH of 5-O-methylvisamminol and to the 11-OH of cimifugin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in N. benthamiana; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (inferred for soluble UGT; not directly stated)
